title: salix bicolor subsp. basaltica
intro: Salix bicolor subsp. basaltica, also known as the basalt willow, is a subspecies of the white willow plant that is native to North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating pain and inflammation, due to its content of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Salix bicolor subsp. basaltica include reducing fever, relieving headaches and menstrual cramps, as well as acting as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. Salix bicolor subsp. basaltica can be prepared for herbal use in various forms, including infusions, tinctures, and salves, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health issues.
